violations.category: Equipment, Food Contact Surfaces, and Utensils Clean
violations.observation: Observed quaternary ammonium compound solution utilized to sanitize food contact surfaces failing to meet parameters to adequately achieve sanitization. Location: sanitation bucket Concentration: 0 ppm facility was using floor cleaner as sanitizer Temperature: 78 °F instructed pic to not use floor cleaner as sanitation and to use quaternary sanitizer that's at the dispensing machine and to always test sanitation with test strips to verify the chemical that's being dispensed and used to wipe down food and non-food contact surfaces Pic remade sanitation bucket in front of inspector and tested properly) Observed equipment food-contact surface with the buildup of soil residues. Equipment Type: Can Opener Location: Kitchen/prep area Condition of the Location: debris buildup on blade where can opener opens cans.

- Observed pests in the facility with no control methods in place. Location: wall near ice machine and wall near electrical panel in back area Pest Type: Beetles Pest Condition: dead Insects and other pests are capable of transmitting disease to humans by contaminating food and food-contact surfaces. Effective measures must be taken to eliminate their presence in food establishments. The premises shall be maintained free of insects, rodents, and other pests. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests shall be controlled to eliminate their presence on the premises by using methods such as trapping devices or other means of pest control. Instructed PIC to remove any dead pests or pest fecal material and to have a professional pest control company set traps and perform treatment as needed. Facility is to provide EHD with appropriate pest control records.

Observed ready-to-eat time and temperature controlled for safety food prepared and held within the establishment with no date marking indicators. Location: cooking area Food Items: All Cold Holding food in cold holding unit Expiration Dates: no dates observed.

PIC has corrected all noted and observed violations. PIC has started maintaining temp logs and calibration logs. All food employees have obtained Food Handler Cards. PIC is schedule to take Food Protection Manager Certification. PIC has provided all photo documentation of corrections and repairs. CHPD inspector has no more cause for concern. Violations corrected: 1. Cold Holding: Time/Temperature Control for Safety Food, Cold Holding. 2. Food Contact Surfaces Cleaned & Sanitized: Sanitizers, Criteria-Chemicals 3. ROYAL HYDERABAD - 3305 JUAN TABO BLVD NE - Unsatisfactory Re-Inspection required ROYAL HYDERABAD - 3305 JUAN TABO BLVD NE Food Establishment Class C Date: 04/22/2026 - #: CAC-Food-038916-2026 - Inspection Result: Unsatisfactory Re-Inspection required Facility has completed in person walk through of violation corrections by the environmental health department to be upgraded into unsatisfactory status. The facility has Corrected the following: - Controlling Pests, If Present: Treatment - Pest control has treated the facility and sealed problem areas. - Removing Dead or Trapped Birds, Insects, Rodents and other Pest Dead rodents have been removed. Found minimal droppings under dish washer. Unsatisfactory sticker was placed on front door Facility will be permitted to re-open under a Grade of Unsatisfactory and will be subject to a follow-up inspection within five (5) business days of reopening and operating. Previous inspection report with highlights of violations that still need to be followed up on was printed and provided to person in charge.
